SAAR Overview SAAR p-value 95% Confidence Interval (CI) SAAR Percentile SAAR Baselines Troubleshooting Additional Resources 
SAAR Overview The Standardized Antimicrobial Administration Ratio (SAAR) provides a standardized metric of antimicrobial use. The SAAR is a ratio comparing observed, or reported, antimicrobial use to the antimicrobial use predicted by a referent, or baseline, population.
A SAAR < 1.0 indicates antimicrobial use was less than predicted. 
A SAAR = 1.0 indicates antimicrobial use was equivalent to predicted use.
